title: msqrob2PTM: DIA-NN case study - Paired phospho-enriched and non-enriched samples
text: Mutations tRNAs can lead mis-incorporation amino acid growing polypeptide chain differs specified mRNA process known mistranslation. better understand impact mistranslating tRNA variants, Berg et al.  profiled proteome phosphoproteome yeast expressing three different mistranslating tRNAs. first proline tRNA G3:U70 base pair acceptor stem mis-incorporates alanine proline codons (Pro -> Ala; Hoffman et al. 2017). two serine tRNAs either UGG proline UCU arginine anticodon mis-incorporate serine proline (Pro -> Ser) serine arginine codons (Arg -> Ser), respectively (Berg et al. 2017, 2019b). Six replicates strain grown. replicate non-enriched (whole proteome) phospho-peptide-enriched sample prepared quantified using data independent acquisition workflow. data non-enriched phospho-enriched samples deposited PRIDE, dataset identifiers PXD068388 PXD068392). Summary: Mutations tRNAs can lead mis-incorporation amino acids protein translation. 4 strains: WT, 3 strains mutations leading miss-incorporations (Pro -> Ala, Pro -> Ser Arg -> Ser). 6 biological repeats per strain biological repeat: non-enriched phospho-enriched MS run. title: msqrob2PTM: DIA-NN case study - Paired phospho-enriched and non-enriched samples
text: load msqrob2 package, along additional packages data manipulation visualisation.
code: suppressPackageStartupMessages({ library("QFeatures")   library("dplyr")  library("tidyr") library("ggplot2") library("msqrob2")     library("stringr") library("ExploreModelMatrix") library("MsCoreUtils")  library("matrixStats") library("patchwork") library("kableExtra") library("ComplexHeatmap") library("purrr") library("tibble") library("scater") library("BiocFileCache") })

title: msqrob2PTM: DIA-NN case study - Paired phospho-enriched and non-enriched samples
text: load output DIA-NN parquet files. Can file path local file url file lives web. use BiocFileCache need download files time script knit. can import report.parquet file using read_parquet function arrow package. subset precursor files reduce memory footprint keep variables need downstream analysis. Note Protein.Sites refers exact modified residue position full protein sequence, useful assessing PTM-level quantification. Note, also filter strain ProSer variant . Garbage collection free space
code: bfc <- BiocFileCache() # for usage in local rstudio session on local machine precursorFilePTM <- bfcrpath(bfc,"https://zenodo.org/records/20414816/files/Phosphoproteome_DIANNreport.parquet?download=1") precursorsPTM <- arrow::read_parquet(precursorFilePTM) # function from the arrow package #precursors <- data.table::fread(precursorFile) # For older versions the results are stored as tsv files. precursorsPTM <- precursorsPTM |>    filter(!grepl("ProSer",Run)) |>   select(          Run,           Precursor.Id,           Modified.Sequence,           Stripped.Sequence,           Precursor.Charge,           Protein.Group,           Protein.Names,          Protein.Ids,          Protein.Sites, #exact modified residue position in the full protein sequence          Genes,          Precursor.Quantity,          Precursor.Normalised,          Normalisation.Factor,          Ms1.Area,          Ms1.Normalised,          PG.MaxLFQ,          Q.Value,           Lib.Q.Value,          PG.Q.Value,          Lib.PG.Q.Value,          Proteotypic,          Decoy, # Not available in older versions of DIA-NN          RT) gc(); gc() ##            used  (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb)  max used  (Mb) ## Ncells 10100704 539.5   17846594 953.2  13415964 716.5 ## Vcells 31952126 243.8   92591811 706.5 113387882 865.1 ##            used  (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb)  max used  (Mb) ## Ncells 10100862 539.5   17846594 953.2  13415964 716.5 ## Vcells 31952497 243.8   92591811 706.5 113387882 865.1

title: msqrob2PTM: DIA-NN case study - Paired phospho-enriched and non-enriched samples
text: DIA-NN phosphorylation indicated (UniMod:21) sequence stored variable Modified.Sequence. variable contains precursor sequence along modifications. , keep precursors phosphorylated. Garbage collection free space
code: precursorsPTM <- precursorsPTM |>   filter(grepl(pattern = "\\(UniMod:21\\)", Modified.Sequence)) gc(); gc() ##            used  (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb)  max used  (Mb) ## Ncells 10081752 538.5   17846594 953.2  13415964 716.5 ## Vcells 30255814 230.9   92591811 706.5 113387882 865.1 ##            used  (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb)  max used  (Mb) ## Ncells 10081759 538.5   17846594 953.2  13415964 716.5 ## Vcells 30255860 230.9   92591811 706.5 113387882 865.1

title: msqrob2PTM: DIA-NN case study - Paired phospho-enriched and non-enriched samples
text: Quick check distribution precursors MS2 intensities.  Seems imputation done.
code: precursorsPTM |>    filter(is.finite(Precursor.Quantity) & Precursor.Quantity > 0) |>   ggplot(aes(x = log2(Precursor.Quantity))) +   geom_density() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(subtitle = "Abundances (non-enriched)") + precursorsPTM |>    filter(is.finite(Precursor.Quantity) & Precursor.Quantity > 0) |>   ggplot(aes(x = log2(Precursor.Quantity))) +   geom_density() +   theme_minimal() +   labs(subtitle = "Abundances (phospho-enriched)") #Garbage collection to free space  gc(); gc() ##            used  (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb)  max used  (Mb) ## Ncells 10320151 551.2   17846594 953.2  13415964 716.5 ## Vcells 53503328 408.2   92806745 708.1 113387882 865.1 ##            used  (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb)  max used  (Mb) ## Ncells 10320161 551.2   17846594 953.2  13415964 716.5 ## Vcells 53503380 408.2   92806745 708.1 113387882 865.1

text: sample annotation table available can generated run labels, researchers included information design filenames. Extract unique Run labels Split Run label variables using “_” separator Rename variable Run runCol (needed readQFeatures function) Add sampleId Sort annotation file according sampleId
code: annotPTM <- precursorsPTM |>     dplyr::distinct(Run) |>  ## 1.   separate(Run,            into=c("label1","label2","strain","rep","type","acquisition"),            sep="_",            remove=FALSE) |>   ## 2.   rename(runCol = Run) |> ## 3.   mutate(sampleId = paste0(strain,rep)) |> #4.   arrange(sampleId) #5. annotPTM ## # A tibble: 18 × 8 ##    runCol                  label1 label2 strain rep   type  acquisition sampleId ##    <chr>                   <chr>  <chr>  <chr>  <chr> <chr> <chr>       <chr>    ##  1 e14488_MB057_ArgSer_01… e14488 MB057  ArgSer 01    Phos… DIA         ArgSer01 ##  2 e14491_MB057_ArgSer_02… e14491 MB057  ArgSer 02    Phos… DIA         ArgSer02 ##  6 e14512_MB057_ArgSer_06… e14512 MB057  ArgSer 06    Phos… DIA         ArgSer06 ##  7 e14486_MB057_Ctrl_01_P… e14486 MB057  Ctrl   01    Phos… DIA         Ctrl01   ##  8 e14494_MB057_Ctrl_02_P… e14494 MB057  Ctrl   02    Phos… DIA         Ctrl02   ##  9 e14497_MB057_Ctrl_03_P… e14497 MB057  Ctrl   03    Phos… DIA         Ctrl03   ## 10 e14501_MB057_Ctrl_04_P… e14501 MB057  Ctrl   04    Phos… DIA         Ctrl04   ## 11 e14507_MB057_Ctrl_05_P… e14507 MB057  Ctrl   05    Phos… DIA         Ctrl05   ## 12 e14513_MB057_Ctrl_06_P… e14513 MB057  Ctrl   06    Phos… DIA         Ctrl06   ## 13 e14489_MB057_ProAla_01… e14489 MB057  ProAla 01    Phos… DIA         ProAla01 ## 14 e14493_MB057_ProAla_02… e14493 MB057  ProAla 02    Phos… DIA         ProAla02 ## 15 e14499_MB057_ProAla_03… e14499 MB057  ProAla 03    Phos… DIA         ProAla03 ## 16 e14502_MB057_ProAla_04… e14502 MB057  ProAla 04    Phos… DIA         ProAla04 ## 17 e14506_MB057_ProAla_05… e14506 MB057  ProAla 05    Phos… DIA         ProAla05 ## text: Authors: Lieven Clement1, Nina Demeulemeester2, Luca De Corso3, Lucas Beerland4  Last modified: May 20, 2026.

title: testws
text: Mass spectrometry–based proteomics allows identification quantification myriad posttranslational modifications (PTMs), reveal additional complexity diversity proteome. Indeed, PTMs greatly extend number different forms protein, .e. proteoforms, can found. importantly, PTMs can impact protein functions often act key switches many cellular pathways play vital roles e.g. cell proliferation, metastasis ageing. Novel multiple open-modification search engines developed proteomics community boost identification post-translational modifications (PTMs) mass spectrometry (MS) based technologies. However, despite advances PTM identification, statistical methods sensitive PTM-level quantification differential analysis lagging behind, many researchers continue rely ad-hoc analysis workflows due lack clear guidelines, can lead violations key statistical assumptions. workshop, offer hands-introduction PTM analysis msqrob2 package, provides set rigorously validated benchmarked statistical workflows MS-based proteomics. begin data exploration highlight (1) peptides can contain multiple PTMs refer peptide modifications peptidoforms, (2) inferring individual PTMs requires one summarise peptidoforms carry particular PTM one PTM expression value, show remains important evaluate visualize peptidoforms contribute PTM rule reporting differential PTMs stem subset differential peptidoforms carrying another PTM; (3) parent proteins PTMs occur can also change abundance regardless PTMs. Hence, changes abundance PTM confounded changes protein abundance. therefore introduce concepts Differential Peptidoform/PTM Abundance (DPA) well Differential Peptidoform/PTM Usage (DPU) enable clear distinction directly assessing DA peptidoforms/PTMs (DPA), differences relative usage peptidoforms/PTMs corrected changes overall abundance corresponding protein (DPU). Next, walk participants minimal data processing steps required prior statistical modelling, explaining step necessary. Next, introduce steps required perform DPA DPU. DPU analyses, consider two different experimental designs. illustrate entire workflow peptidoform protein-level abundance acquired sample (paired design). Thereafter show analysis differs peptidoform protein-level abundances quantified independent samples (unpaired design). Finally, demonstrate translate biological questions hypothesis tests prioritise, report visualise peptidoforms well PTMs differentially abundant / differentially used across treatment groups upon stimulation.
